info.title: OMDb API
info.description:
The Open Movie Database — film metadata keyed by IMDb id, exact title, or free-text title search. Returns release year, genres, director, runtime, plot summary, and IMDb rating. Used by the movie pack as the canonical source for film facts.

OMDb is a single endpoint that switches behaviour on which query parameter you pass: `i` (IMDb id) → fetch by id, `t` (title) → fetch first match by title, `s` (search term) → list matches. Always pass exactly ONE of `i`, `t`, or `s`.

get.summary: Look up a movie by IMDb id, exact title, or search term.
get.description:
Pass ONE of `i`, `t`, or `s`:
- `i: tt0110912` — fetch the single record for that IMDb id (cheapest, most precise; prefer this whenever you have an id).
- `t: 'Pulp Fiction'` — fetch the first movie OMDb matches on title (use `y` to disambiguate remakes).
- `s: 'pulp'` — search for up to 10 titles containing the term; pick the right `imdbID` from the result and follow up with another call passing `i`.

Response shape varies by mode. With `i` or `t` you get a `MovieDetail`. With `s` you get a `SearchResult` (an array of `SearchHit`s in `Search` plus `totalResults`). Both shapes set `Response: 'True'` on hit, `Response: 'False'` with an `Error` field on miss — OMDb returns HTTP 200 either way, so check `Response` rather than the status code.

parameters.name: i
parameters.description: IMDb id including the leading 'tt', e.g. 'tt0110912' for Pulp Fiction. Use when you already know the id (cheapest call).

parameters.name: t
parameters.description: Movie title for an exact-ish single-record lookup. Case-insensitive; OMDb does light fuzzy matching. Combine with `y` to disambiguate.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: Search term — substring of the title. Returns up to 10 hits per page; cap pagination at 1–3 pages.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Release year filter, four-digit string. Useful with `t` (disambiguate remakes) and `s` (narrow search).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Restrict to a media type. Default is any.

parameters.name: plot
parameters.description: Only meaningful with `i` or `t`. 'short' (default) is one sentence; 'full' is a paragraph.

200.description: On `i` or `t`: `MovieDetail`. On `s`: `SearchResult`. Both set `Response: 'False'` with an `Error` field on miss.

MovieDetail.description: Full OMDb record for one title. Returned when the request used `i` or `t`. `Response: 'False'` indicates a miss.

Year.description: Four-digit year; for series, may be a range like '2005–2013'.

Runtime.description: Runtime as 'NNN min' string. Strip ' min' before parsing.

imdbRating.description: IMDb 1–10 rating as a string, e.g. '8.9'. Parse to float.

imdbID.description: Stable IMDb id including the 'tt' prefix. Use this as Movie.imdbId when persisting.

Error.description: Present only when Response is 'False', e.g. 'Movie not found!'.

SearchHit.description: One row of a search result. To get full metadata, follow up with another call passing `i: hit.imdbID`.

SearchResult.description: Paginated search response. Returned when the request used `s`. `Response: 'True'` with a `Search` array on hit, `Response: 'False'` with `Error` on miss.

totalResults.description: Total hit count as a string. Parse to int for display.
